results.rendered_html:
abhi-√grah -gṛhṇāti, to take hold of, take up (from the soil),  TS.  &c.;
to accept, receive,  MBh. ;
to set (as a blossom),  BhP. ;
to lay together, to fold (the hands) See abhigṛhīta-pāṇi below:
Caus. -grāhayati, to catch, surprise, e.g. rūpāBigrAhita, taken in the very act,  Daś.

results.rendered_html:
abhigrah (Ved. grabh) 9 U.
1 To take or seize, catch, catch hold of, seize forcibly, attack; ripuṇābhigṛhya  Dk. 5 , 98 , 103. 
2 To accept, take.
3 To receive, (as a guest).
4 To fold, lay or bring together (as the hands).
5 To set, show or bring forth (as blossom, fruit &c.).
— Caus. To catch or surprise one in the very act, to let oneself be so caught; aryavaryasya kasyacid gṛhe corayitvā rūpābhigrahito baddhaḥ  Dk. 96.
